steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: Examine the terms $7x^5$, $-5x^4$, and $9x^3$. The coefficients (7, 5, 9) share no common factor. The powers of $x$ are $x^5$, $x^4$, and $x^3$, so the greatest common factor is $x^3$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
When we factor out $x^3$ from each term:
  • $7x^5 \div x^3 = 7x^2$
  • $-5x^4 \div x^3 = -5x$
  • $9x^3 \div x^3 = 9$

Therefore: $7x^5 - 5x^4 + 9x^3 = x^3(7x^2 - 5x + 9)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
